title: Procris visciformis
taxonomy: Procris visciformis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Moraceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The exact classification within the Moraceae family is not specified in available data. Procris visciformis is sometimes placed in the genus Maoutia or the genus Procris, depending on the source. Further research is required to determine the most accurate classification.
uses: Procris visciformis is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various ailments. Its leaves and roots are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. In some cultures, Procris visciformis is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. The plant's extracts are also believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses for Procris visciformis.
